summary: Tested BotanIQ backend (15/17 pytest passing) and frontend (Expo web preview). All non-AI backend endpoints work (Plants CRUD, QR lookup, Readings + status logic, Summary, Delete cascade, no _id leaks). Both AI vision endpoints (POST /api/analyze/meter and /api/analyze/health) return HTTP 502 because the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY hit a 'Budget has been exceeded' error from the LiteLLM provider — this is an INFRASTRUCTURE/KEY issue, NOT a code bug. The vision code path, model wiring (gemini-3-flash-preview), and JSON extraction are implemented correctly. Frontend renders perfectly on 390x844 mobile viewport: Dashboard (summary tiles + plant grid), Add Plant modal (validation works), Scan hub (Health/Meter/QR mode toggle + capture), Plant Detail (hero image, status, QR code BIQ-XXXXXXXX, metrics grid, timeline), Activity tab. End-to-end add-plant flow tested: create -> auto-navigate to detail screen with correct id & qr_code.

critical.issue: Returns 502: LiteLLM 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0' from EMERGENT_LLM_KEY. Code path is correct — provider/budget needs to be refreshed.
Same Budget exceeded error from LiteLLM. Same root cause as /analyze/meter.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents' (cosmetic, from a child component — non-blocking).

action_items: Refresh / top-up the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY budget (provider returns 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0'). Verify with: curl -X POST $URL/api/analyze/health -d '{"image_base64":"<jpeg b64>"}' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' — once budget is available, both /analyze/* endpoints should return 200 with structured JSON.
Optional: replace deprecated `pointerEvents` prop with `style={{pointerEvents:'...'}}` to silence React Native Web warning.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 405 lines — under the 700-line threshold, no split needed yet.
_strip_mongo_id helper is defined but unused; all queries already use projection {_id:0}. Safe.
_llm_vision strips data-URI prefix correctly — base64 input is robust.
Reading status logic correctly maps moisture<35 -> 'thirsty' and fertility<30 -> 'needs_fertilizer'. Verified via tests.
DELETE /api/plants/{id} correctly cascades to readings + analyses collections.

success_rate.backend: 88% (15/17 passed; 2 AI tests blocked by LLM key budget, not code)

seed_data_creation: Created and deleted TEST_Monstera and TEST_UI_Basil during tests; cleanup performed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — re-run with: cd /app/backend && pytest tests/backend_test.py -v. Once LLM budget is restored, the 2 failing AI tests should pass without any code changes. Frontend testIDs to use: dashboard-screen, add-plant-button, plant-card-{id}, add-plant-screen, close-add-plant, input-name, input-species, input-location, save-plant-button, plant-detail-screen, plant-qr-code, log-reading-button, back-button, delete-plant-button, scan-hub-screen, mode-health, mode-meter, mode-qr, capture-button, capture-from-library.
rca_of_issue: AI endpoints 502: LiteLLM (used internally by emergentintegrations.LlmChat) raised litellm.BadRequestError -> OpenAIException 'Budget has been exceeded! Current cost: 0.0, Max budget: 0.0'. Reproduction: POST a valid JPEG base64 to /api/analyze/health with EMERGENT_LLM_KEY set — server logs show the full litellm traceback in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log. Mitigation: increase / reset the budget on the EMERGENT_LLM_KEY account. No code change required.
